title: Event model and payload
purpose: Define the Event enum — the spine of the event-sourcing system — and the Payload type that carries type-erased values through it. Every recorded fact in a workflow's life is an Event variant.
task: Define a Payload newtype carrying opaque serialised bytes plus a content-type tag, with lossless conversion to and from serde_json::Value. Define the Event enum with all lifecycle, activity, timer, signal, and child-workflow variants, each wrapped in an envelope carrying a per-workflow sequence number, a recorded timestamp, and the owning WorkflowId. Event must be type-erased — it carries Payload values, never a generic type parameter.

requirements.title: Payload newtype
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL define Payload as a newtype over a byte buffer plus a content-type tag. THE SYSTEM SHALL provide lossless conversion between Payload and serde_json::Value.
acceptance: payload.rs defines Payload holding bytes and a content-type tag (an enum or stable string identifying the encoding).
Payload::from_json(&serde_json::Value) and Payload::to_json() -> Result round-trip losslessly for all JSON value kinds.
Payload derives Serialize, Deserialize, Clone, Debug, PartialEq.
A unit test asserts round-trip fidelity for null, bool, number, string, array, and object.

requirements.title: Event envelope
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL define an event envelope carrying a monotonic per-workflow sequence number, a recorded UTC timestamp, and the owning WorkflowId. Every event SHALL carry this envelope.
acceptance: event.rs defines an envelope (a struct field group or wrapper) with seq: u64, recorded_at: DateTime<Utc>, and workflow_id: WorkflowId.
The envelope is part of every Event value, accessible via accessors regardless of variant.
The recorded_at timestamp is the documented determinism source for workflow.now (a doc comment states this).
A unit test constructs an event and reads back its seq, recorded_at, and workflow_id.

requirements.title: Event variants
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL define the Event enum with workflow-lifecycle, activity, timer, signal, and child-workflow variants. Variants carrying user data SHALL carry it as Payload, and the enum SHALL NOT take a generic type parameter.
acceptance: Workflow lifecycle: WorkflowStarted (workflow_type + input Payload), WorkflowCompleted (result Payload), WorkflowFailed (WorkflowError), WorkflowCancelled (reason).

Activity: ActivityScheduled (ActivityId + activity_type + input Payload), ActivityStarted (ActivityId), ActivityCompleted (ActivityId + result Payload), ActivityFailed (ActivityId + ActivityError + attempt).
Child workflow: ChildWorkflowStarted (child WorkflowId + workflow_type + input), ChildWorkflowCompleted (child WorkflowId + result), ChildWorkflowFailed (child WorkflowId + WorkflowError).
Event has no generic type parameter; all user data is Payload.

requirements.title: Event serialisation round-trip
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL derive Serialize and Deserialize on Event and all its variants. Serialising an Event and deserialising it back SHALL produce an equal value.
acceptance: Event derives Serialize, Deserialize, Clone, Debug, PartialEq.
A unit test round-trips at least one event of every variant through serde_json and asserts equality.
Enum representation is explicit (tagged) so the wire form is stable and forward-readable.

boundaries: SHALL NOT compute WorkflowStatus from events — that is AC-003.

SHALL NOT define ActivityError or WorkflowError bodies beyond referencing them — their definitions land in AC-004 (this brief may use a minimal placeholder only if AC-004 is not yet merged, but the final shape is owned by AC-004).
SHALL NOT define any storage trait.
verification: No #[allow], #[expect], or #[ignore] directives in new code.
